{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Kikuma no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 =
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = Kukumata clan?
|家祖 = {{ill|Ōka-kuni-no-Atai|ja|大鹿国直}}
|種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}})
|出身地 =
|根拠地 = Kikuma Province<br />(later {{ill|Ichihara District|ja|市原郡}}, {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Muzashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|无邪志国造}}<br />{{ill|Ōshima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|大島国造}}<br />{{ill|Hōki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伯岐国造}}
}}
'''Kikuma no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''kikuma no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kukuma no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kikuma kokuzō'', ''kukuma kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled [[#Territory|Kikuma Province]] (the northwestern part of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
*According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, '''{{ill|Ōka-kuni-no-Atai|ja|大鹿国直}}''', son of {{ill|Etamohi-no-Mikoto|ja|兄多毛比命}} — the ancestor of the {{ill|Muzashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|无邪志国造}} — was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
Possibly '''Kukumata clan''' (''kukumata-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Miyatsuko|ja|造}}). Of the same lineage as the {{ill|Muzashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|无邪志国造}} ({{ill|Munazashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|胸刺国造}}), {{ill|Hōki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|波伯国造}}, and {{ill|Ōshima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|大島国造}}.

== Base ==
The base of the kuni no miyatsuko is said to have been at '''{{ill|Kikuma-gō|ja|菊麻郷}}''' (present-day Kikuma in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|ja|市原市|lt=Ichihara|lt_ja=市原市}}) in {{ill|Ichihara District|ja|市原郡}}. The {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} {{ill|provincial capital|ja|国府|lt=provincial capital|lt_ja=国府}} was later set up in Ichihara District.

=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Kikuma Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|Ichihara District|ja|市原郡}} of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}, equivalent to the basin of the {{ill|Murata River|ja|村田川}} from the northern part of present-day Ichihara City to the southern part of {{ill|Chiba|ja|千葉市|lt=Chiba City|lt_ja=千葉市}}. The southern boundary of Ichihara District extended to the {{ill|Yōrō River|ja|養老川}}. At {{ill|Kikuma Kofun Cluster|ja|菊間古墳群}} in Kikuma-gō of the same district, numerous {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} remain that are considered to be tombs of the kuni no miyatsuko class of that era.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Unknown, as there are no records of {{ill|Engishiki-listed shrines|ja|式内社}} in Ichihara District in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}''.

=== Tombs ===
*{{ill|Kikuma Kofun Cluster|ja|菊間古墳群}}
*:A {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} from the middle to late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|ja|市原市|lt=Ichihara|lt_ja=市原市}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, consisting of one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun (rectangle)|ja|前方後方墳}}, four {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}}, and large {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}}.
*{{ill|Ōmaya Futagozuka Kofun|ja|大厩二子塚古墳}}
*:A 71-m-long keyhole-shaped kofun located to the southeast of the Kikuma Kofun Cluster. Built in the mid-Kofun period.

== People ==
*'''{{ill|Ōka-kuni-no-Atai|ja|大鹿国直}}''' (''ōka-kuni-no-atai'', also Ōsaka-nushi-no-Atai)
*:The first {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}, appearing in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''.
*'''Ojika-no-Atai''' (''ojika-no-atai'')
*:Son of Ōka-kuni-no-Atai.

=== Women ===
*'''Otohime'''
*:Became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and bore Homuyawake-no-Miko.

== References ==
*''Nihon-shi Sōran'' ({{ill|Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}})

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

== External links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kikuma.html Walking the Kofun of Bōsō: Kofun of Kikuma Province]

{{DEFAULTSORT:きくまのくにののみやつこ}}
[[Category:上総国]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:菊麻国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
[[Category:Translated but not moved]]
